concerns.text: FR-001 (≥100 repos) and SC-001 (≥95% completeness) lack a dedicated task to save the cleaned dataset and validate the threshold. T011 is marked [ ] (incomplete) and describes the exact missing step: 'Save cleaned dataset... AND validate ≥95% completeness'. Without this task, the pipeline output is orphaned and the success criterion is unverified.

FR-002 (log-transform) and US-2 Acceptance Scenario 2 (fit quality metrics for BOTH log-normal and Weibull) are missing implementation tasks. T016 (fitting models) is marked [ ] (incomplete). T015 only covers ECDF plots. There is no task to perform the MLE fitting and report KS/AIC for the distribution families.

US-2 Acceptance Scenario 3 (report extreme outliers >30 days and percentage) is missing an implementation task. T017 is marked [ ] (incomplete). No other task describes detecting outliers or calculating their percentage of the dataset.

SC-004 (Model predictive performance via LOO-CV) and US-3 Acceptance Scenario 2 (MAE/R² metrics) are missing implementation tasks. T023 is marked [ ] (incomplete). No other task implements the leave-one-repository-out cross-validation logic required to generate these metrics.

FR-007 (sensitivity analysis sweeping {0.01, 0.05, 0.1}) is partially covered by T025, but the task description lacks the explicit requirement to sweep the specific thresholds mandated by the spec. The task says 'sweeping a range of cutoffs' without defining the set {0.01, 0.05, 0.1}, risking partial coverage.

Constitution Principle II (Verified Accuracy) requires the Reference-Validator Agent to run as a blocking gate on the `research_review` → `research_accepted` transition. T039 implements the agent, but no task exists to *execute* this validation logic on the artifacts generated in Phases 3-5 before the transition. The plan requires execution, not just implementation.

FR-008 (associational language) requires the phrase in 'all result text'. T026 enforces this in `code/analysis/results.py`, and T041 validates result text. However, no task explicitly ensures that *generated figures* (created in T018) include the 'associational' disclaimer in their captions or titles, which falls under 'all result text' per the spec's intent.

T005 implements the API client but does not explicitly mention the '≥60 seconds' wait logic required by US-1 Acceptance Scenario 3 and FR-001's rate limit handling. While 'exponential backoff' is mentioned, the specific 60-second constraint is missing from the task description, creating a potential gap in traceability.
